title: brassica fruticulosa subsp. radicata
intro: Brassica fruticulosa subsp. radicata, also known as Wild Cabbage or Land Cress, is a plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing the risk of cancer, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Wild Cabbage can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or added to food as a culinary ingredient, and has been used in traditional medicine for its potential therapeutic effects.
